rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 22, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH LUEBBERKE'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied without prejudice. Court finds that insufficient notice was provided pursuant to C.C.P. Section 1005 based upon the filing date of December 30, 2013. However, Court also notes that, based on its understanding of the facts, the legal authority for plaintiff's motion arises under CCP Section 2032.610, not the general provision of section 2031.210. See also Weil and Brown, et al., CAL. PRAC. GUIDE: CIV. PRO BEFORE TRIAL (The Rutter Group 2013) §§8:1602 et seq. The parties are advised to meet and confer concerning the legal authorities and materials discoverable by plaintiff from all medical examinations prior to re-filing any motion to compel production. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW V. SMITH VS. THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 22, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT KATHLEEN CALLINAN, OPERA PLAZA, LP ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's opposition was untimely, as it was only filed one day before Defendants' reply brief was due. (See CCP sec. 1005(b).) Even considering the late-filed opposition, the demurrer is sustained on the merits. As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") does not plead facts to establish that Plaintiff was a party to the contract. As to the second cause of action for breach of contract, the SAC does not plead facts to establish that Plaintiff was a party to the contract. Additionally, the SAC does not plead facts that establish that the contract was expressly entered into for the benefit of Plaintiff; thus, Plaintiff cannot enforce the contract under a third party beneficiary theory. (See Shutes v. Cheney (1954) 123 Cal.App.2d 256, 262.) Further, Plaintiff provides no legal support for his assertion in his opposition brief that "[a]s a dues paying homeowner, Plaintiff is entitled to enforce the General Contract¿" (Opposition, p. 3.) As to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, the SAC does not plead facts that establish Defendants owed a fiduciary duty to Plaintiffs. As to the fourth cause of action for trespass to real property, the SAC does not plead facts that establish that Defendants entered property possessed or controlled by Plaintiff. (See Civic Western Corp. v. Zila Industries, Inc. (1977) 66 Cal.App.3d 1, 16, "The essence of the cause of action for trespass is an 'unauthorized entry' onto the land of another.") The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has already had two opportunities to amend the complaint to state a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW V. SMITH VS. THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 22, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT THE OPERA PLAZA RESIDENTIAL PARCEL HOMEOWNERS, THE OPERA PLAZA MASTER OWNER'S ASSOCIATION ("MOA") DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part, and overruled in part. As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, the demurrer is overruled. The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately alleges the elements of breach of contract - (1) Plaintiff entered into an agreement with Defendants (SAC, 7-8); (2) per the terms of the agreement, the Board must notice an owner in writing if disciplinary action is to be taken against him (SAC, 14-15); (3) the Board never notified Plaintiff that he would be denied access to the parking garage (SAC, 23); and (4) Plaintiff was damaged by this breach. (SAC, 24.) As to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, the demurrer is overruled. The SAC pleads around the business judgment rule by alleging that Defendants acted "to increase revenues by 25% monthly." (SAC, 30; see Everest Investors 8 v. McNeil Partners (2003) 114 Cal.App.4th 411, 430, "The business judgment rule does not shield actions taken without reasonable inquiry, with improper motives, or as a result of a conflict of interest.") As to the fourth cause of action for trespass, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The SAC does not plead facts that establish that Defendants entered property possessed or controlled by Plaintiff. (See Civic Western Corp. v. Zila Industries, Inc. (1977) 66 Cal.App.3d 1, 16, "The essence of the cause of action for trespass is an 'unauthorized entry' onto the land of another.") The demurrer to this cause of action is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff has already had two opportunities to amend the complaint to state a cause of action. The motion to strike the additional allegations to the third cause of action is denied. Although Plaintiff exceeded the scope of the order granting him leave to amend, given the liberal policy of amendments to pleadings, the Court finds that it serves judicial economy to allow the allegations to remain in the complaint, rather than have Plaintiff file a motion for leave to amend, which would more likely than not be granted. The motion to strike the prayer for punitive damages is granted, as the SAC does not allege that Defendants engaged in malicious or fraudulent conduct. (See Broussea v. Jarrett (1977) 73 Cal.App.3rd 864.) Paragraphs 98 and 100 are stricken from the Second Amended Complaint. rulings.Case Title: CHARNET DENISE MOORE VS. SANMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Order Allowing Deft'S To Conduct Discovery Of Pltf'S Mental Health History
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 22, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANTS SANMO INTERNATIONAL COMPANY INC., GUASHENG HE'S Notice Of Motion For Order Allowing Defendant's To Conduct Discovery Of Plaintiff's Mental Health History Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. The complaint alleges two statutory counts arising from denial of disabled access under the California Disabled Persons Act and the Unruh Civil Rights Act. The plaintiff does not allege a claim for personal injuries or intentional infliction of emotional distress apart from a general damages allegation arising from the statutory claims. Other than establishing that plaintiff is a member of the class of protected persons under the Disabled Persons Act and the Americans with Disabilities Act by virtue of a physical disability, plaintiff does not put her mental condition in issue under the complaint. The authorities cited by the parties are distinguished in that all cases arose in personal injury cases, where a specific waiver of privilege might be found, or, as in the Vinson case, from allegations of intentional infliction of emotional distress and factual allegations causing mental and emotional distress. The Court has not been cited to, or located, any authority that provides a similar waiver in statutory disability access or civil rights cases that do not allege a personal injury tort. Based upon privacy analysis, plaintiff has a strong privacy interest in her mental condition and medical records. The moving party fails to demonstrate adequate necessity or exceptional circumstances to compel discovery into plaintiff's mental condition (see, e.g., CCP Sec. 2032.320 (b)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Form And Special Interrogatories And Deem All Matters Stated In Requests For Admission
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 22, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form And Special Interrogatories And Deem All Matters Stated In Requests For Admission Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part. Verified responses to outstanding interrogatories and Requests for Admission to be provided to opposing party within fifteen (15) days. The motion is denied to the extent that moving party seeks an order for "deemed admissions." The Court agrees that a declaration of service that recites delivery to a messenger would be insufficient evidence of service for the purposes of a motion to compel responses, but attachment of a proof of service to a copy of the propounded discovery is not required. See Code of Civil Procedure §2030.280(b). The purpose of the proof of service is evidentiary to prove that the discovery was served in accordance with the statutes. No significant issue exists that the propounding party did not serve the discovery in accordance with the statutes. However, the parties did not engage in good faith meet and confer discussions concerning an extension of time to respond as opposed to re-service. Re-service of the discovery does not appear warranted absent a claim by responding party that it did not actually receive the propounded discovery. Court finds no substantial justification for the objections and awards sanctions of $800. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories-First Set
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 22, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT JOSEPH ALIOTO, THE ALIOTO LAW FIRM, A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories-First Set Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part as follows: (1) Granted as to Nos. 2, 9, 10, 15 (as modified), 16 (as modified), 26 (as modified), 27, and 28. The Court notes that general objections are not proper in response to interrogatories and are disallowed in their entirety. See Code of Civil Procedure Section 2030.210, 2030.300; Weil and Brown, et al., CAL. PRAC. GUIDE: CIV. PRO. BEFORE TRIAL (The Rutter Group), §§8:1071 et seq. The objections as to "vague and ambiguous" are overruled as to numbers 2, 9, and 10. The objections to Nos. 15, 16, 27, and 28 are overruled, because the Court finds the interrogatories sufficiently definite to define the scope of the interrogatory. However, to avoid any potential ambiguity, nos. 15 and 16 are modified to reference the plaintiff as the recipient and to refer to all payments made by defendants to plaintiff. As to No. 26, the objection is sustained as to the phrase, "anything else," and the interrogatory modified to strike the phrase, "anything else." The balance of the interrogatory is sufficiently intelligible to merit a response. Court finds no substantial justification for the majority of objections and awards sanctions in the amount of $800. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Case Title: YELIZAVETA LIVSHITS VS. TAYLOR K. SMITH MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 22, 2014, Line 17, Defendant Sutter West Bay Hospitals' Demurrer To Complaint The demurrer to the cause of action for elder abuse is sustained without leave to amend because plaintiff fails to state a cause of action against this defendant. See CCP 430.10(e). Plaintiff lumps all of the defendants together and does not allege specific facts regarding defendant hospital¿s wrongdoing. Plaintiff does not allege ¿neglect¿ within the meaning of Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 15610.57. Plaintiff does not allege misconduct distinct from professional negligence. The allegations against defendant hospital do not show a ¿failure of those responsible for attending to the basic needs and comforts of elderly or dependent adults¿ or a ¿failure to provide medical care.¿ Covenant Care, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 783 (emphasis in original). The demurrer to the battery cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff lumps all of the defendants together. Plaintiff does not allege specific facts against the hospital (as opposed to the treating physician) showing how defendant hospital deliberately deviated from the patient¿s consent. rulings.Case Title: SYSCO SA FRANCISCO, INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. IBRAHIM HAMAMI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, January 22, 2014, Line 15, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SA FRANCISCO, INC.,A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER Denied. Plaintiff has not established the probable validity of its claim against Ibrahim Hamami, individually and dba Star Restaurant. (CCP § 484.090.) The open book account identifies the account holder as "Star Restaurant", and not Mr. Hamami. (Ableman Decl., Exhibit A.) Plaintiff has not presented sufficient evidence to demonstrate that Mr. Hamami does business as Star Restaurant. In the First Amended Complaint, Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Hamami signed a personal guaranty. (First Am. Complaint, 14, Exhibit B.) However, Exhibit B is not attached to the First Amended Complaint. Furthermore, the Complaint is not verified as averred in the Application. (Compare Complaint; with Application, 7a, Cook Decl., p. 1:10-11.)
